response:
High level — fazure-mcp is an App Service (azure-mcp-prod) fronted directly by an Azure Application Gateway (with WAF) and uses a combination of network‑level allow‑lists (trusted VPN / known AWS Janus IPs) plus application/OAuth flows for application‑level auth.

Concrete pieces and request flow

- Ingress: client -> Azure Application Gateway (Application Gateway serves Host fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com).
- WAF / listener policies:
  - Global WAF policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax.
  - Per‑listener/site WAF policy for MCP: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites.
  - Custom rules in play: RateLimit, VpnOnly, and a short‑circuit allow created to avoid OWASP managed‑rule inspection for trusted internal traffic:
    - AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp (priority 3) short‑circuits OWASP CRS inspection for Host=fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com and RequestUri /^/mcp for Rackspace VPN IP ranges (and extended to include Janus AWS IPs when required).
    - VpnOnly and other custom rules enforce blocking of non‑trusted traffic.
- Trusted traffic: Rackspace VPN ranges (and an explicit Janus AWS IP set) are allow‑listed so internal clients are not blocked by OWASP SQLi false positives on MCP payloads.
- OAuth / registration flows: oauth/auth flows are handled by a separate host (fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com) and have a similar allow‑list short‑circuit (AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth) to permit internal registration/auth operations.
- Backend app: requests that pass AGW are routed to azure-mcp-prod App Service (probe path /health). App Service (and App Insights) perform application‑level authentication/authorization (token validation, etc.) and business logic.
- Observability & fixes: AGW access/firewall logs are used to detect WAF blocks (e.g. OWASP CRS rule IDs) and the mitigating pattern is to add precise short‑circuit allow rules or per‑rule exclusions rather than disabling managed rules globally.

Summary
- Network ingress and first line of “authentication”/access control is Application Gateway WAF with carefully ordered custom rules and allow‑lists for trusted VPN/Janus IPs.
- Application‑level auth (OAuth/token flows) is handled by the application stack (fazure-oauth + azure-mcp-prod).

authentication debugging pattern → contained_in → APIM", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → contained_in → x-auth-ddi"], "recent": ["2026-04-14: On 2026-04-14, after new MCP 403s from Janus AWS IPs, added the full Janus AWS address set 52.39.2.74, 52.41.136.221, 52.10.115.132, and 52.40.141.99 to the trusted IP allowlists for fazure MCP and oauth endpoints on Azure Application Gateway. Root cause was that recent POST /mcp failures on fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com at 2026-04-14T17:51:41Z through 2026-04-14T18:06:13Z were blocked by custom rule VpnOnly under policy scope fazure-mcp, even though those IPs are the known JanusAWS set already present in the global policy. Fixed live by updating site policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites rules AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and VpnOnly, and global policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax rules AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth and FazureOauthVpnOnly, adding all four Janus AWS IPs to the IPMatch lists.
[specifics: janus_aws_ips: 52.39.2.74, 52.41.136.221, 52.10.115.132, 52.40.141.99; fazure_mcp_hostname: fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com; blocked_method_and_path: POST /mcp; blocked_rule: VpnOnly (policy_scope=fazure-mcp); site_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites; site_policy_rules_updated: AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp; VpnOnly; global_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax; global_policy_rules_updated: AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth; FazureOauthVpnOnly; ipmatch_update: added all four Janus AWS IPs to IPMatch lists of the above rules]", "2026-04-15: On 2026-04-15, investigation of fazure-mcp 500/502/403 traffic found that the current issue was not WAF regression and not backend 500s. In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scanner or preview IPs such as 146.70.189.237 and 52.123.190.89/125. The fazure-mcp backend is App Service azure-mcp-prod in rg-azure-mcp-prod with linked App Insights azure-mcp-prod. App Service state was Running and App Gateway show-backend-health reported the backend healthy. App Service metrics showed Requests and Http2xx continuing through the window and Http5xx stayed 0 throughout, confirming the backend was not returning server 500s. App Gateway metrics showed HealthyHostCount stayed 1 and UnhealthyHostCount stayed 0 throughout the incident while FailedRequests spiked, indicating short transient backend unavailability that did not trip health-state change
[specifics: app_service: azure-mcp-prod (rg-azure-mcp-prod); app_insights: azure-mcp-prod; probe_config: host=azure-mcp-prod.prodengineeringase.p.azurewebsites.net; path=/health; interval=300s; timeout=30s; unhealthyThreshold=3; other_probes_interval: 30s; backend_http_setting: fazure-mcp-backend; protocol=HTTPS; port=443; requestTimeout=20s; workers: numberOfWorkers=1; health_counts: HealthyHostCount=1; UnhealthyHostCount=0; http5xx: Http5xx=0; manual_restart: daniel.temalatzi@rackspace.com from 72.32.180.178; cert_issue: managed certificate renewal failed because DNS pointed to App Gateway IP=20.118.70.227 instead of App Service IP]", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated fazure-mcp WAF 403s for POST /mcp from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181. Local Azure-MCP rollout artifacts showed the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites was created by copying managedRules and policySettings from the global policy, then attaching only site custom rules RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, and AllowSafeFilters. The global RackspaceVPN Allow rule was not copied into the site policy. Result: trusted VPN traffic still traverses OWASP CRS 3.2 managed rules on /mcp and legitimate MCP SQL request bodies are likely blocked as SQLi false positives. Recommended fix is either a listener-policy custom Allow rule for trusted VPN /mcp traffic or a narrower per-rule exclusion on the JSON arg carrying SQL text after confirming the exact RuleId in AGWFirewallLogs.
[specifics: waf_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites; attached_custom_rules: RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, AllowSafeFilters; missing_rule: RackspaceVPN Allow; managed_rules_version: OWASP CRS 3.2; source_ip: 72.32.180.181; endpoint: POST /mcp; likely_blocked: legitimate MCP SQL request bodies due to SQLi false positives; recommended_fix_allow: listener-policy custom Allow rule for trusted VPN /mcp traffic; recommended_fix_exclusion: narrower per-rule exclusion on JSON arg carrying SQL text (confirm RuleId in AGWFirewallLogs); log_source: AGWFirewallLogs]", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, a reusable WAF mitigation pattern was established for trusted internal MCP SQL traffic behind Azure Application Gateway. Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 was receiving 403s on POST /mcp to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com because the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites allowed the VPN through the VpnOnly rule but still sent the JSON request body through OWASP managed inspection. AGWFirewallLogs showed SQL injection false positives on ARGS:params.arguments.query with rules 920230, 942100, 942150, 942260, 942360, 942370, 942380, 942410, 942480, then final 949110 anomaly score block. Root cause was that the dedicated site WAF policy copied managed rules but did not include an allow-short-circuit for trusted Rackspace VPN traffic to the MCP endpoint. Fixed live by creating custom allow rule AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp on listener policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites at priority 3, after RateLimit at 1 and VpnOnly at 2. The rule matches Host fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com and later also fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com, RequestUri beginning /mcp, and Rackspace VPN IP ranges. This short-circuits m
[specifics: waf_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites; custom_allow_rule: AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp (priority=3); rule_priority_order: RateLimit=1, VpnOnly=2, AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp=3; mcp_hosts: fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com; fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com; request_uri_prefix: /mcp; rackspace_vpn_ip: 72.32.180.181; owasp_rule_ids: 920230, 942100, 942150, 942260, 942360, 942370, 942380, 942410, 942480, 949110; agw_log_sqli_field: ARGS:params.arguments.query (SQL injection false positives); transaction: d01bb9fed06bbc7fd2c1e28c031119eb (HTTP 200)]", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.178 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/register. Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly score block. Updated global WAF custom rules by moving BlockKnownPath from priority 5 to 6 and adding AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth at priority 5 for Host=fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com, RequestUri regex ^/(\.well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(?:/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)$, and Rackspace VPN IP ranges. This bypasses managed WAF inspection and the generic authorize/token path blocker for trusted internal MCP OAuth registration/auth flows.
